content: A curiosity-driven autonomous agent can leverage an intrinsic curiosity signal from its ICM/RND framework as a natural weighting mechanism for online meta-learning memory consolidation, where high-curiosity (novel, surprising) experiences are prioritized for retention to mitigate catastrophic forgetting, while low-curiosity (overlearned, redundant) observations are gradually pruned. This creates a self-regulating memory system that aligns what the agent intrinsically wants to learn with what it needs to retain for future few-shot adaptation.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
